slides.title: Tell It Another Way
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 5 English, Unit 7: Tell It Another Way. Welcome to Unit 7. This unit is called "Tell It Another Way" because you will explore one of the most powerful tools a writer can use: point of view. Every story is told by someone, and the person telling the story decides what the reader sees, hears, and feels. When you change the narrator, the whole story can feel completely different, even if the events stay exactly the same. Think about a football match. If the winning team tells the story, they might say, "We played brilliantly and scored three goals!" If the losing team tells the same story, they might say, "It was a difficult day. We tried our best but could not find the net." The match is the same, but the feelings, the details, and the mood are completely different. That is the power of... In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Explain what point of view means and identify the narrator in a text
Compare two versions of the same event told from different perspectives and explain what changes
Describe how changing the narrator changes the mood, detail, and feeling of a story
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to explain what point of view means and identify the narrator in a text. you will also compare two versions of the same event told from different perspectives and explain what changes. you will also describe how changing the narrator changes the mood, detail, and feeling of a story. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: local, urban, rural, spacious, continent, colonial. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Tell It Another Way: source text 1
Tell It Another Way: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Tell It Another Way: source text 1 and Tell It Another Way: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Does It Mean to Express an Opinion?
bullets: An opinion is what you think or believe about something. It is not a fact — it is your own view. Different people can have different opinions about the...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Does It Mean to Express an Opinion?. An opinion is what you think or believe about something. It is not a fact — it is your own view. Different people can have different opinions about the same thing, and that is perfectly fine. Sharing our opinions helps us have good discussions, understand each other, and learn new ideas. A fact is something that can be proved true or false. An opinion is something that someone thinks or feels. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1: Retell a Story from a Different Point of View
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Hot-Seating (Character Interview). Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
